title: IDF Kills Three Hamas Commanders in Gaza Strikes, Including Oct 7 Nukhba Participant
detail: On April 16, 2026, the IDF announced it had killed three Hamas commanders in separate targeted strikes in Gaza during the prior week. The eliminated commanders included: Islam Hisham Riyad Kanita and Mahmoud Hamed Youssef Hamdouna, both commanders in Hamas's Production Unit responsible for rebuilding rocket and weapons manufacturing capabilities; and Ahmed Abu Khdeira, a commander in Hamas's communications unit in the Gaza City area. Separately, the IDF confirmed it had also killed Ali Sami Mohammad Shakra, a Nukhba unit commander who participated directly in the October 7, 2023 kidnapping of Israeli hostages from Kibbutz Re'im, and who had been planning imminent attacks against Israeli forces. The Nukhba force was Hamas's elite special operations unit responsible for leading the October 7 breach operations and hostage-taking. The IDF stated it used precise munitions and aerial surveillance to minimize civilian casualties. The Gaza Government Media Office reported that Israel had committed more than 2,400 violations of the October 2025 ceasefire agreement, including strikes, arrests, and blockades. The Foundation for Defense of Democracies separately documented 14 Hamas and other Palestinian faction violations of the ceasefire between April 8–16.

media.caption: IDF kills three Hamas commanders in Gaza this week, including Oct 7 Nukhba participant

title: Six Months Into Gaza Ceasefire: 'Recovery Hasn't Even Begun,' Residents Say
detail: On April 16, 2026 — marking approximately six months since the October 2025 Gaza ceasefire — NPR published a major assessment documenting the lack of meaningful recovery in Gaza despite the truce. Residents described living among rubble with no functioning infrastructure, inadequate food and medicine supplies, and no visible reconstruction progress. The report noted that international reconstruction funding remained almost entirely frozen: less than $1 billion of the $17 billion pledged to the Board of Peace had been deposited, as donor nations hesitated due to the ongoing Iran-Israel regional conflict and Hamas's refusal to accept disarmament. No international peacekeeping force had been deployed, and the Palestinian Authority had failed to establish administrative control over Gaza. Humanitarian organizations warned that without full Phase One compliance by Israel — including restoration of supply lines and return of displaced Palestinians — conditions in Gaza would continue to deteriorate despite the nominal ceasefire.

media.caption: Six months into Gaza ceasefire: residents say recovery hasn't even begun — NPR
